close_reason: Split into 5 granular tickets: troveler-2hc (NOT operator), troveler-7ub (schema), troveler-yo9 (tag command), troveler-5r2 (search integration), troveler-8li (update preservation)
description:
To group the tools into meaningful categories (because terminal trove itself only has attributes like licence and language) we are going to introduce tags.
Tags have more flexibility than creating a strict taxonomy
## Proposed Solution
We will add a tag command with add, remove, list and clear subcommands
Tags will be need to added to search: tag keyword, multiples allowed e.g.:
"(name=foo&tag=file&tag=ai&!tag=search)"
DB schema will need to be adapted (don't worry about the current state, you can drop the db if needed)
Probably smarter to first implement the ! (not) operator and then the tags.
We need to make sure that running `troveler update` does not delete any tags we may have added
Everything requires tests!!!
title: [FEATURE] Tagging system for tools

close_reason: Implemented NOT operator: added FilterNot type, tokenizer/parser support, SQL generation, installed filter handling, and comprehensive tests
description:
Add support for negating filter expressions in search queries.

## Current State
Search supports: field=value, & (AND), | (OR), parentheses
NOT supported: ! (negation) operator

## Requirements
1. Extend filter parser to tokenize ! prefix on terms
2. Add Not type to Filter AST in db/models.go
3. Update filter_sql.go to generate NOT / NOT EXISTS clauses
4. Support both simple (!field=value) and complex (!(a|b)) negations

## Examples
- !installed=true - show uninstalled tools
- !language=go - exclude Go tools
- !(language=go|language=rust) - exclude Go and Rust

## Tests
- Unit tests for parser
- Unit tests for SQL generation
- Integration tests with real search
title: Implement NOT (!) operator for search filters

title: Review: Fix tag ID generation

close_reason: Split into 6 separate tickets with proper dependencies
description:
Review findings from troveler-2qx. All items should be addressed before building CLI commands (troveler-yo9) or tag preservation (troveler-8li).

## Critical: Enable foreign key enforcement
SQLite does NOT enforce foreign keys by default. `ON DELETE CASCADE` in `tool_tags` is a no-op right now. Add to `New()`:
```go
db.SetMaxOpenConns(1)
db.Exec("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON")
```
This directly affects tag preservation during updates — cascades won't fire without it.

## High: Fix tag ID generation
`AddTag` builds IDs via `fmt.Sprintf("tag-%s", tagName)` — raw user input in primary keys. Breaks with spaces, unicode, mixed case. Normalize input (`strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(tagName))`) and either:
- (A) Drop `tags.id`, key by `name TEXT PRIMARY KEY COLLATE NOCASE`, or
- (B) Keep `id` but generate opaque IDs (hash/random), keep `name UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE`

Option A is simplest since `name` is already UNIQUE and all lookups use it.

## High: Fix API asymmetry (slug vs toolID)
- `AddTag/RemoveTag/ClearTags` accept **slug**
- `GetTags` accepts **toolID**

This leaks internals — callers (and tests) must know ID construction. Add `GetTagsBySlug(slug)` or change `GetTags` to accept slug. All tag APIs should use the same key.

## Medium: Prune orphaned tags
`RemoveTag` and `ClearTags` delete from `tool_tags` but leave the `tags` row. `GetAllTags` will surface dead tags with count=0. Either:
- Prune after unlink: `DELETE FROM tags WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT tag_id FROM tool_tags)`
- Or document it as intentional (global vocabulary)

## Medium: Validate/normalize tag names on input
No trimming or normalization — `"cli"`, `" cli "`, `"CLI"` become 3 separate tags. Add `strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(...))` + empty check at the top of `AddTag`.

## Low: Test improvements
- Tests hardcode `"tool-fzf"` as toolID — use slug-based API instead
- Unchecked `AddTag` errors in setup (lint warnings)
- Missing edge cases: spaces in tag names, unicode, case-insensitive dedup, FK cascade behavior
- `seedTool` returns `*Tool` that's never used (unparam lint)
title: Refine: Tags schema correctness and API consistency

description:
Enable searching tools by their tags using tag= filter syntax.

## Requirements
1. Extend filter parser to recognize tag= as valid field
2. Update filter_sql.go to JOIN tool_tags table
3. Support multiple tags with AND/OR: tag=cli&tag=fuzzy
4. Support negation: !tag=experimental

## Examples
```troveler search tag=cli
troveler search "tag=cli&tag=fuzzy"
troveler search "(tag=cli|tag=tui)&!tag=experimental"
troveler search "language=go&tag=ai"
```

## Implementation Notes
- JOIN with tool_tags and tags tables
- Handle NOT tag with LEFT JOIN / NOT EXISTS
- Support combining tag filters with existing fields

## Depends On
- NOT operator ticket (for !tag= support)
- Tags schema ticket (for DB structure)
- Tag command ticket (for test data)

## Tests
- Parser tests for tag= syntax
- SQL generation tests
- Integration tests with tagged tools
title: Add tag= filter to search queries

close_reason: Added tags schema: tags and tool_tags tables, Tag/TagCount models, and DB methods (GetTags, GetAllTags, AddTag, RemoveTag, ClearTags, GetToolsByTag). All tests pass.
description:
Create database tables for user-curated tool tags.

## Schema Design
```sql
CREATE TABLE tags (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    name TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE tool_tags (
    tool_id TEXT REFERENCES tools(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    tag_id TEXT REFERENCES tags(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    PRIMARY KEY (tool_id, tag_id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_tool_tags_tag_id ON tool_tags(tag_id);
```

## Implementation
1. Add tables in db/sqlite.go:createTables()
2. Add DB methods:
   - GetTags(toolID) []string
   - GetAllTags() []Tag (for autocomplete/listing)
   - AddTag(slug, tagName) error
   - RemoveTag(slug, tagName) error
   - ClearTags(slug) error
   - GetToolsByTag(tagName) []Tool

## Notes
- Tags are user-curated, NOT scraped from terminaltrove.com
- Can drop existing DB for clean migration
- Tests for all DB operations
title: Add tags database schema (many-to-many)

close_reason: Implementation complete. Tags are preserved during updates via GetAllTagsBySlug/ReapplyTags pattern. All tests pass.
description:
Ensure user-curated tags survive database updates from terminaltrove.com.

## Problem
The update command upserts tools and may change internal IDs. User tags must be preserved.

## Solution
1. Before update: snapshot all tool_tags (by slug, since slug is stable)
2. After upsert: re-apply tags to tools using slug matching
3. Handle tools that no longer exist in remote (keep orphan tags? or clean up?)

## Implementation
- Modify commands/update.go to wrap DB writes with tag preservation
- Store map[slug][]tag before update starts
- After all tools upserted, re-apply tags

## Edge Cases
- Tool removed from terminaltrove.com: tags stay in DB (orphan cleanup optional)
- Slug renamed: tags lost (acceptable, user can re-tag)
- Tag deleted but tool_tags reference exists: foreign key cascade

## Tests
- Add tags to tools, run update, verify tags still present
- Test with tools added/removed from mock data
title: Preserve user tags during troveler update

description:
Currently we only show what we've parsed from the TT entry in terms of install methods.
But we have install platforms/methods that are derived from the original data e.g. mise go:, mise cargo: but now also mise github (from eget)
The table in the install command should be a union of parsed and generated commands. The platform names for the mise method are simply mise:$backend, so mise:cargo, mise:npm, mise:pipx (even if we're using uv under the hood) and mise:github (even though it's parsed from eget)
Split this ticket if more efficient / necessary

close_reason: Refinement complete - fixed typo, replaced regex with strings.CutPrefix, added edge case tests
description:
There are occasions where you encounter no useful installation method but at least an eget command.
Turns out eget just pulls a release from github. We can do the same thing with mise with the github backend.
eget foo/bar
is the same as
mise use --global github:foo/bar
So if there's an eget command you can offer a virtual mise command (which is a mise fallback if we don't have a specific language command)
title: Transform eget commands

Fixed CLI override priority issue. When mise mode is enabled (via --mise flag or config), CLI --override flag now properly overrides the LANG behavior.

description:
When mise_mode is enabled (from config or --mise flag), the CLI --override flag should still be respected. Currently, if mise mode is on, any -o flag is ignored because cliOverride is forced to 'LANG' before checking the CLI value.

Fix: Only force cliOverride='LANG' when mise mode is enabled AND no CLI override was provided by the user. CLI parameters should have higher priority than config settings.
title: Fix: CLI override should have higher priority than mise mode

close_reason:
Fixed virtual platform override handling. Added ResolveVirtualPlatform() to map mise:* platforms back to their source platforms (mise:github → github, mise:go → go, etc.).

Changes:
- commands/install.go: Added ResolveVirtualPlatform() and updated override logic
- commands/install_test.go: Added comprehensive tests for virtual platform resolution

Now -o mise:github, -o mise:go, -o mise:cargo all work correctly.
description:
When using --override with virtual platforms like mise:github or mise:go, the command selection fails because it only looks for raw platforms.

Virtual platforms are generated for display but the override matching logic doesn't know how to resolve them back to their source platforms (mise:github → github, mise:go → go).

Fix: Add virtual platform resolution in override logic to map mise:* platforms back to their source platforms before matching.
title: Fix: Override should handle virtual mise:* platforms

description:
Fix code quality issues identified during review of tag= filter implementation.

## Defects to Fix

1. **db/filter_sql.go:89** - Line too long (124 chars, max 120)

2. **db/filter_sql.go:45** - Missing blank line before return

3. **db/sqlite.go** - Unchecked error returns for rows.Close()

### Code Quality (SHOULD FIX)

5. **Test files** - String literals as constants

description:
When there's a tool with eget as a platfrom the mise:github platform gets generated (virtual platform).
But trying to use `troveler installer -o mise:github ffuf` just returns "No install command found for github" where '-o eget' returns the mise command.
This is unexpected behaviour (especially since it's not immediately evident that the platforms are related.
## Expected Behavior
-o eget selects the eget command -o mise:github selects the mise:github command
title: [BUG] mise:github platform not selectable

close_reason: Enabled FK enforcement in New() with SetMaxOpenConns(1) + PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON. Added TestForeignKeyCascade.
description:
SQLite does NOT enforce foreign keys by default. ON DELETE CASCADE in tool_tags is a no-op right now. Add to New():

```go
db.SetMaxOpenConns(1)
db.Exec("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON")
```

This directly affects tag preservation during updates — cascades won't fire without it.
title: Enable SQLite foreign key enforcement

sorted by published descending, default limit 10

## Feature Description
--only-totw - toggle that filters for tooloftheweek mark, 
--lang [rust,go,python,...] - option for filtering by language
--limit n - option is obvious and applies do post-filter not pre-filter record set
## Motivation
terminaltrove.com also has it and it's g

This might need splitting into subtasks depending on what's already there.
title: [FEATURE] Newest command

close_reason: Implemented tag command with add/remove/list/clear subcommands. Added --json flag for list output. Unit tests cover all subcommands and error cases.
description:
Add troveler tag command for managing user-curated tags on tools.

## Subcommands
```
troveler tag add <slug> <tag>      # Add tag to tool
troveler tag remove <slug> <tag>   # Remove tag from tool
troveler tag list [slug]           # List all tags (or tags for specific tool)
troveler tag clear <slug>          # Remove all tags from tool
```

## Examples
```troveler tag add fzf fuzzy
troveler tag add fzf cli
troveler tag list fzf    # Output: fuzzy, cli
troveler tag list        # Output: all tags with counts
troveler tag remove fzf cli
troveler tag clear fzf
```

## Implementation
1. Create commands/tag.go with Cobra subcommands
2. Wire up to db methods from schema ticket
3. Output formatting (plain text, JSON with --json flag)
4. Error handling for non-existent tools/tags

## Tests
- Unit tests for each subcommand
- Error cases (tool not found, tag not found, duplicate tag)
title: Implement tag command with CRUD subcommands
